tools.name: click
tools.description: Clicks on the provided element

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

dblClick.description: Set to true for double clicks. Default is false.

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: close_page
tools.description: Closes the page by its index. The last open page cannot be closed.

pageId.description: The ID of the page to close. Call list_pages to list pages.

tools.name: drag
tools.description: Drag an element onto another element

from_uid.description: The uid of the element to drag

to_uid.description: The uid of the element to drop into

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: emulate
tools.description: Emulates various features on the selected page.

networkConditions.description: Throttle network. Omit to disable throttling.

cpuThrottlingRate.description: Represents the CPU slowdown factor. Omit or set the rate to 1 to disable throttling

geolocation.description: Geolocation (`<latitude>x<longitude>`) to emulate. Latitude between -90 and 90. Longitude between -180 and 180. Omit clear the geolocation override.

userAgent.description: User agent to emulate. Set to empty string to clear the user agent override.

colorScheme.description: Emulate the dark or the light mode. Set to "auto" to reset to the default.

viewport.description: Emulate device viewports '<width>x<height>x<devicePixelRatio>[,mobile][,touch][,landscape]'. 'touch' and 'mobile' to emulate mobile devices. 'landscape' to emulate landscape mode.

tools.name: evaluate_script
tools.description:
Evaluate a JavaScript function inside the currently selected page. Returns the response as JSON,
so returned values have to be JSON-serializable.

items.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

args.description: An optional list of arguments to pass to the function.

tools.name: fill
tools.description: Type text into a input, text area or select an option from a <select> element.

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: fill_form
tools.description: Fill out multiple form elements at once

uid.description: The uid of the element to fill out

elements.description: Elements from snapshot to fill out.

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: get_console_message
tools.description: Gets a console message by its ID. You can get all messages by calling list_console_messages.

msgid.description: The msgid of a console message on the page from the listed console messages

tools.name: get_network_request
tools.description: Gets a network request by an optional reqid, if omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

reqid.description: The reqid of the network request. If omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

requestFilePath.description: The absolute or relative path to save the request body to. If omitted, the body is returned inline.

responseFilePath.description: The absolute or relative path to save the response body to. If omitted, the body is returned inline.

tools.name: handle_dialog
tools.description: If a browser dialog was opened, use this command to handle it

action.description: Whether to dismiss or accept the dialog

promptText.description: Optional prompt text to enter into the dialog.

tools.name: hover
tools.description: Hover over the provided element

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: lighthouse_audit
tools.description: Get Lighthouse score and reports for accessibility, SEO and best practices. This excludes performance. For performance audits, run performance_start_trace

outputDirPath.description: Directory for reports. If omitted, uses temporary files.

tools.name: list_console_messages
tools.description: List all console messages for the currently selected page since the last navigation.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of messages to return. When omitted, returns all requests.

pageIdx.description: Page number to return (0-based). When omitted, returns the first page.

types.description: Filter messages to only return messages of the specified resource types. When omitted or empty, returns all messages.

includePreservedMessages.description: Set to true to return the preserved messages over the last 3 navigations.

tools.name: list_network_requests
tools.description: List all requests for the currently selected page since the last navigation.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of requests to return. When omitted, returns all requests.

pageIdx.description: Page number to return (0-based). When omitted, returns the first page.

resourceTypes.description: Filter requests to only return requests of the specified resource types. When omitted or empty, returns all requests.

includePreservedRequests.description: Set to true to return the preserved requests over the last 3 navigations.

tools.name: list_pages
tools.description: Get a list of pages  open in the browser.

tools.name: navigate_page
tools.description: Go to a URL, or back, forward, or reload. Use project URL if not specified otherwise.

type.description: Navigate the page by URL, back or forward in history, or reload.

ignoreCache.description: Whether to ignore cache on reload.

handleBeforeUnload.description: Whether to auto accept or beforeunload dialogs triggered by this navigation. Default is accept.

initScript.description: A JavaScript script to be executed on each new document before any other scripts for the next navigation.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

tools.name: new_page
tools.description: Open a new tab and load a URL. Use project URL if not specified otherwise.

url.description: URL to load in a new page.

background.description: Whether to open the page in the background without bringing it to the front. Default is false (foreground).

isolatedContext.description: If specified, the page is created in an isolated browser context with the given name. Pages in the same browser context share cookies and storage. Pages in different browser contexts are fully isolated.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

tools.name: performance_analyze_insight
tools.description: Provides more detailed information on a specific Performance Insight of an insight set that was highlighted in the results of a trace recording.

insightSetId.description: The id for the specific insight set. Only use the ids given in the "Available insight sets" list.

insightName.description: The name of the Insight you want more information on. For example: "DocumentLatency" or "LCPBreakdown"

tools.name: performance_start_trace
tools.description: Start a performance trace on the selected webpage. Use to find frontend performance issues, Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS), and improve page load speed.

reload.description: Determines if, once tracing has started, the current selected page should be automatically reloaded. Navigate the page to the right URL using the navigate_page tool BEFORE starting the trace if reload or autoStop is set to true.

autoStop.description: Determines if the trace recording should be automatically stopped.

filePath.description: The absolute file path, or a file path relative to the current working directory, to save the raw trace data. For example, trace.json.gz (compressed) or trace.json (uncompressed).

tools.name: performance_stop_trace
tools.description: Stop the active performance trace recording on the selected webpage.

filePath.description: The absolute file path, or a file path relative to the current working directory, to save the raw trace data. For example, trace.json.gz (compressed) or trace.json (uncompressed).

tools.name: press_key
tools.description: Press a key or key combination. Use this when other input methods like fill() cannot be used (e.g., keyboard shortcuts, navigation keys, or special key combinations).

key.description: A key or a combination (e.g., "Enter", "Control+A", "Control++", "Control+Shift+R"). Modifiers: Control, Shift, Alt, Meta

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: resize_page
tools.description: Resizes the selected page's window so that the page has specified dimension

tools.name: select_page
tools.description: Select a page as a context for future tool calls.

pageId.description: The ID of the page to select. Call list_pages to get available pages.

bringToFront.description: Whether to focus the page and bring it to the top.

tools.name: take_memory_snapshot
tools.description: Capture a heap snapshot of the currently selected page. Use to analyze the memory distribution of JavaScript objects and debug memory leaks.

filePath.description: A path to a .heapsnapshot file to save the heapsnapshot to.

tools.name: take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the page or element.

format.description: Type of format to save the screenshot as. Default is "png"

quality.description: Compression quality for JPEG and WebP formats (0-100). Higher values mean better quality but larger file sizes. Ignored for PNG format.

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot. If omitted takes a pages screenshot.

fullPage.description: If set to true takes a screenshot of the full page instead of the currently visible viewport. Incompatible with uid.

filePath.description: The absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory, to save the screenshot to instead of attaching it to the response.

tools.name: take_snapshot
tools.description:
Take a text snapshot of the currently selected page based on the a11y tree. The snapshot lists page elements along with a unique
identifier (uid). Always use the latest snapshot. Prefer taking a snapshot over taking a screenshot. The snapshot indicates the element selected
in the DevTools Elements panel (if any).

verbose.description: Whether to include all possible information available in the full a11y tree. Default is false.

filePath.description: The absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory, to save the snapshot to instead of attaching it to the response.

tools.name: type_text
tools.description: Type text using keyboard into a previously focused input

submitKey.description: Optional key to press after typing. E.g., "Enter", "Tab", "Escape"

tools.name: upload_file
tools.description: Upload a file through a provided element.

uid.description: The uid of the file input element or an element that will open file chooser on the page from the page content snapshot

filePath.description: The local path of the file to upload

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: wait_for
tools.description: Wait for the specified text to appear on the selected page.

text.description: Non-empty list of texts. Resolves when any value appears on the page.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

tools.name: mem_capture_passive
tools.description:
Extract and save structured learnings from text output. Use this at the end of a task to capture knowledge automatically.

The tool looks for sections like "## Key Learnings:" or "## Aprendizajes Clave:" and extracts numbered or bulleted items. Each item is saved as a separate observation.

Duplicates are automatically detected and skipped — safe to call multiple times with the same content.

content.description: The text output containing a '## Key Learnings:' section with numbered or bulleted items

tools.name: mem_context
tools.description: Get recent memory context from previous sessions. Shows recent sessions and observations to understand what was done before.

project.description: Filter by project (omit for all projects)

scope.description: Filter observations by scope: project (default) or personal

tools.name: mem_get_observation
tools.description: Get the full content of a specific observation by ID. Use when you need the complete, untruncated content of an observation found via mem_search or mem_timeline.

tools.name: mem_save
tools.description:
Save an important observation to persistent memory. Call this PROACTIVELY after completing significant work — don't wait to be asked.

WHEN to save (call this after each of these):
- Architectural decisions or tradeoffs
- Bug fixes (what was wrong, why, how you fixed it)
- New patterns or conventions established
- Configuration changes or environment setup
- Important discoveries or gotchas
- File structure changes

FORMAT for content — use this structured format:
  **What**: [concise description of what was done]
  **Why**: [the reasoning, user request, or problem that drove it]
  **Where**: [files/paths affected, e.g. src/auth/middleware.ts, internal/store/store.go]
  **Learned**: [any gotchas, edge cases, or decisions made — omit if none]

TITLE should be short and searchable, like: "JWT auth middleware", "FTS5 query sanitization", "Fixed N+1 in user list"

Examples:
  title: "Switched from sessions to JWT"
  type: "decision"
  content: "**What**: Replaced express-session with jsonwebtoken for auth\n**Why**: Session storage doesn't scale across multiple instances\n**Where**: src/middleware/auth.ts, src/routes/login.ts\n**Learned**: Must set httpOnly and secure flags on the cookie, refresh tokens need separate rotation logic"

  title: "Fixed FTS5 syntax error on special chars"
  type: "bugfix"
  content: "**What**: Wrapped each search term in quotes before passing to FTS5 MATCH\n**Why**: Users typing queries like 'fix auth bug' would crash because FTS5 interprets special chars as operators\n**Where**: internal/store/store.go — sanitizeFTS() function\n**Learned**: FTS5 MATCH syntax is NOT the same as LIKE — always sanitize user input"

scope.description: Scope for this observation: project (default) or personal

session_id.description: Session ID to associate with (default: manual-save-{project})

title.description: Short, searchable title (e.g. 'JWT auth middleware', 'Fixed N+1 query')

topic_key.description: Optional topic identifier for upserts (e.g. architecture/auth-model). Reuses and updates the latest observation in same project+scope.

type.description: Category: decision, architecture, bugfix, pattern, config, discovery, learning (default: manual)

tools.name: mem_save_prompt
tools.description: Save a user prompt to persistent memory. Use this to record what the user asked — their intent, questions, and requests — so future sessions have context about the user's goals.

session_id.description: Session ID to associate with (default: manual-save-{project})

tools.name: mem_search
tools.description: Search your persistent memory across all sessions. Use this to find past decisions, bugs fixed, patterns used, files changed, or any context from previous coding sessions.

query.description: Search query — natural language or keywords

scope.description: Filter by scope: project (default) or personal

type.description: Filter by type: tool_use, file_change, command, file_read, search, manual, decision, architecture, bugfix, pattern

tools.name: mem_session_end
tools.description: Mark a coding session as completed with an optional summary.

tools.name: mem_session_start
tools.description: Register the start of a new coding session. Call this at the beginning of a session to track activity.

tools.name: mem_session_summary
tools.description:
Save a comprehensive end-of-session summary. Call this when a session is ending or when significant work is complete. This creates a structured summary that future sessions will use to understand what happened.

FORMAT — use this exact structure in the content field:

## Goal
[One sentence: what were we building/working on in this session]

## Instructions
[User preferences, constraints, or context discovered during this session. Things a future agent needs to know about HOW the user wants things done. Skip if nothing notable.]

## Discoveries
- [Technical finding, gotcha, or learning 1]
- [Technical finding 2]
- [Important API behavior, config quirk, etc.]

## Accomplished
- ✅ [Completed task 1 — with key implementation details]
- ✅ [Completed task 2 — mention files changed]
- 🔲 [Identified but not yet done — for next session]

## Relevant Files
- path/to/file.ts — [what it does or what changed]
- path/to/other.go — [role in the architecture]

GUIDELINES:
- Be CONCISE but don't lose important details (file paths, error messages, decisions)
- Focus on WHAT and WHY, not HOW (the code itself is in the repo)
- Include things that would save a future agent time
- The Discoveries section is the most valuable — capture gotchas and non-obvious learnings
- Relevant Files should only include files that were significantly changed or are important for context

content.description: Full session summary using the Goal/Instructions/Discoveries/Accomplished/Files format

tools.name: mem_suggest_topic_key
tools.description: Suggest a stable topic_key for memory upserts. Use this before mem_save when you want evolving topics (like architecture decisions) to update a single observation over time.

content.description: Observation content used as fallback if title is empty

title.description: Observation title (preferred input for stable keys)

type.description: Observation type/category, e.g. architecture, decision, bugfix

tools.name: mem_update
tools.description: Update an existing observation by ID. Only provided fields are changed.

tools.name: create-note

vault.description: Name of the vault to create the note in

filename.description: Just the note name without any path separators (e.g. 'my-note.md', NOT 'folder/my-note.md'). Will add .md extension if missing

content.description: Content of the note in markdown format

folder.description: Optional subfolder path relative to vault root (e.g. 'journal/subfolder'). Use this for the path instead of including it in filename

tools.name: list-available-vaults
tools.description: Lists all available vaults that can be used with other tools

tools.name: edit-note
tools.description:
Edit an existing note in the specified vault.

    There is a limited and discrete list of supported operations:
    - append: Appends content to the end of the note
    - prepend: Prepends content to the beginning of the note
    - replace: Replaces the entire content of the note

Examples:
- Root note: { "vault": "vault1", "filename": "note.md", "operation": "append", "content": "new content" }
- Subfolder note: { "vault": "vault2", "filename": "note.md", "folder": "journal/2024", "operation": "append", "content": "new content" }
- INCORRECT: { "filename": "journal/2024/note.md" } (don't put path in filename)

tools.name: search-vault

vault.description: Name of the vault to search in

query.description: Search query (required). For text search use the term directly, for tag search use tag: prefix

path.description: Optional subfolder path within the vault to limit search scope

caseSensitive.description: Whether to perform case-sensitive search (default: false)

searchType.description: Type of search to perform (default: content)

tools.name: move-note
tools.description: Move/rename a note while preserving links

vault.description: Name of the vault containing the note

source.description: Source path of the note relative to vault root (e.g., 'folder/note.md')

destination.description: Destination path relative to vault root (e.g., 'new-folder/new-name.md')

tools.name: create-directory
tools.description: Create a new directory in the specified vault

vault.description: Name of the vault where the directory should be created

path.description: Path of the directory to create (relative to vault root)

recursive.description: Create parent directories if they don't exist

tools.name: delete-note
tools.description: Delete a note, moving it to .trash by default or permanently deleting if specified

vault.description: Name of the vault containing the note

path.description: Path of the note relative to vault root (e.g., 'folder/note.md')

reason.description: Optional reason for deletion (stored in trash metadata)

permanent.description: Whether to permanently delete instead of moving to trash (default: false)

tools.name: add-tags

vault.description: Name of the vault containing the notes

files.description: Array of note filenames to process (must have .md extension)

normalize.description: Whether to normalize tag format (e.g., ProjectActive -> project-active) (default: true)

position.description: Where to add inline tags in content (default: end)

tools.name: remove-tags

vault.description: Name of the vault containing the notes

files.description: Array of note filenames to process (must have .md extension)

tags.description: Array of tags to remove (without # symbol). Example: ['project', 'work/active']

location.description: Where to remove tags from (default: both)

normalize.description: Whether to normalize tag format (e.g., ProjectActive -> project-active) (default: true)

preserveChildren.description: Whether to preserve child tags when removing parent tags (default: false)

patterns.description: Tag patterns to match for removal (supports * wildcard) (default: [])

tools.name: rename-tag

vault.description: Name of the vault containing the tags

oldTag.description: The tag to rename (without #). Example: 'project' or 'work/active'

newTag.description: The new tag name (without #). Example: 'projects' or 'work/current'

createBackup.description: Whether to create a backup before making changes (default: true)

normalize.description: Whether to normalize tag names (e.g., ProjectActive -> project-active) (default: true)

batchSize.description: Number of files to process in each batch (1-100) (default: 50)

tools.name: read-note

vault.description: Name of the vault containing the note

filename.description: Just the note name without any path separators (e.g. 'my-note.md', NOT 'folder/my-note.md')

folder.description: Optional subfolder path relative to vault root

resources.name: Available Vaults
resources.description: List of all available Obsidian vaults and their access status

resources.name: obsidian-vault
resources.description: Access information for the obsidian-vault vault

tools.name: resolve-library-id
tools.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns a list of matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'get-library-docs' to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID.

tools.name: get-library-docs
tools.description: Fetches up-to-date documentation for a library. You must call 'resolve-library-id' first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query. Use mode='code' (default) for API references and code examples, or mode='info' for conceptual guides, narrative information, and architectural questions.

context7CompatibleLibraryID.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

mode.description: Documentation mode: 'code' for API references and code examples (default), 'info' for conceptual guides, narrative information, and architectural questions.

page.description: Page number for pagination (start: 1, default: 1). If the context is not sufficient, try page=2, page=3, page=4, etc. with the same topic.
